title: Intrinsic TGF-β signaling attenuates proximal tubule mitochondrial injury and inflammation in chronic kidney disease
type1_summary: The Excel workbook is figure-organized source data with tabular, replot-ready measurements for multiple figures, matching the paper’s source-data statements.
type2_summary: The PDF source-data files and the large two-column TXT files appear to be raw or near-raw supporting data requiring substantial preprocessing before plotting.
classification_notes: The key distinction is that 'source data' in Nature can still be Type 1 if it is figure-organized and tabular, as with the XLSX workbook. The PDF files are treated conservatively as Type 2 because they are not directly replot-ready and likely contain raw or semi-raw supporting material. The TXT files are large identifier-to-count tables and are also conservatively Type 2.

file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, very large size (166.7 MB), and source_data provenance indicate a compiled source-data document rather than a clean tabular figure-ready dataset. Large PDF source-data files in Nature often contain raw images or multi-panel supporting material.
file_classifications.reasoning: Although it is a source-data deposit, the file is not a clean replot-ready table. Its size and PDF format suggest it contains raw or minimally processed figure evidence rather than directly plottable tabular values. Conservatively classify as raw/unprocessed.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Moderate: without page-level inspection, the exact contents are unknown, but the size and format strongly favor raw/source material over cleaned tabular data.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: PDF document; no tabular structure exposed

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies how intrinsic TGF-β signaling in proximal tubule (PT) cells affects chronic kidney disease progression using a mouse aristolochic acid (AA) injury model, spatial transcriptomics, bulk RNA-seq, microscopy, and biochemical assays. The main finding is that PT-specific deletion of Tgfbr2 worsens mitochondrial injury, impairs complex I/mitochondrial quality control, shifts metabolism toward aerobic glycolysis, and amplifies macrophage/dendritic-cell-driven Th1 inflammation and fibrosis; human CKD snRNA-seq data show decreased TGF-β receptor expression and impaired PT metabolism, supporting a protective role for intact PT TGF-β signaling.
measurement_types: Histology (H&E, Sirius red, Oil Red O)

figures.description: Workflow for AA injury, Visium integration/annotation, and identification of major renal cell types in kidney slices.
Spatial plots showing impaired cortico-medullary organization in injured kidneys compared with uninjured kidneys.
Cell-type proportion analysis across conditions, showing remodeling and altered proportions of PT and interstitial/immune cells.
Spatial transcriptomics browser images showing PT injury and fibrosis markers (Havcr1, Vcam1, Col1a1) at baseline and 3 weeks after AA.
H&E images of kidneys from uninjured and 6-week AA-injured mice showing tubular injury and infiltrate.
RT-qPCR quantification of Kim-1 mRNA in renal cortex after AA injury.
FACS quantification of CD45+ leukocyte infiltration in injured kidneys.
Sirius red staining showing cortical collagen accumulation/fibrosis.
Quantification of Sirius red-positive fibrotic area.
Blood urea nitrogen (BUN) levels after AA injury.
TEM images of proximal tubule mitochondria showing injury features in γGT-Cre;Tgfbr2fl/fl mice.
Quantification of mitochondrial length and mitochondrial injury score from TEM images.
Multiphoton live microscopy/TMRM images and quantification of mitochondrial membrane potential in PTs.
figures.likely_source: raw measurement for images; derived for quantification

figures.description: Oil Red O staining and quantification of cortical lipid deposition.
Metacore pathway analysis of PT DEGs highlighting ubiquinone metabolism and complex I-related changes.
Immunoblot of OXPHOS proteins showing reduced NDUFB8 in renal cortex.
Cell fractionation immunoblot and quantification showing reduced mitochondrial NDUFB8 in TβRII−/− PT cells.
NAD+/NADH ratio measurement in PT cells.
DCF-based ROS measurement by FACS in PT cells.
Effects of NAD+ treatment on ROS, ATP, and lactate production in PT cells.
RT-qPCR of Pgc1α mRNA in renal cortex under uninjured and AA conditions.
Pgc1α immunohistochemistry and quantification of positive nuclei.
mito-QC reporter fluorescence images and quantification of mitolysosome/mitophagic flux.
LC3A immunoblot and quantification in renal cortex.
Metacore pathway analysis of injured PT DEGs showing innate immunity/complement/IFNγ pathways.
FACS quantification of CD4+ and CD8+ T cells in kidneys after AA injury.
FACS quantification of IFNγ+ and TNFα+ CD4/CD8 T cells.
Cgas and Sting immunoblotting and quantification in renal cortex.
UMAP clustering of human kidney PT snRNA-seq data.
Proportion of PT cells per cluster in healthy vs CKD biopsies.
Feature plots showing TGFBR1/2/3 expression in healthy and CKD PT cells.
Differential expression showing decreased TGFBR1/2/3 and TGFBRAP1 in CKD PT cells.
Pathway activity analysis showing impaired mitochondrial biogenesis, complex I activity, and metabolism in CKD PT.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from kidney tissue and PT cells, including histology images (H&E, Sirius red, Oil Red O), transmission electron microscopy images of mitochondria, multiphoton/TMRM live imaging, immunofluorescence/IHC images, western blots, flow cytometry events, RT-qPCR Ct-based measurements, Seahorse OCR/ECAR metabolic flux measurements, and bioluminescence assay readouts for ATP, lactate, and NAD+/NADH. It also uses spatial transcriptomics capture data and human snRNA-seq database data, but the paper text does not specify file formats or repository structure.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper contains many processed/derived plots: spatial transcriptomics cluster maps and cell-type proportions, gene-expression overlays, pathway enrichment analyses (Metacore/EnrichR/REACTOME), quantified histology areas, quantified immunoblot densitometry, quantified FACS counts, and statistical summaries (bar plots with mean ± SEM). These are downstream analyses derived from raw images, sequencing counts, or cytometry measurements.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The text repeatedly states 'Source data are provided as a Source data file' for figures, indicating figure-level source data availability. It also mentions that Visium dataset integration used the GSE151658 database and that snRNAseq analyses used a kidney biopsy database of healthy and CKD patients, but the excerpt does not include a formal data availability section or repository links for all datasets.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of experimental modalities and instruments: kidney histology sections, TEM micrographs, multiphoton/TMRM images, flow cytometry measurements, immunoblots, RT-qPCR, Seahorse metabolic assays, spatial transcriptomics, and human snRNA-seq database analyses. The excerpt does not specify raw file formats, accession numbers for all datasets, or whether raw sequencing/cytometry/image files are deposited.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include integrated Visium cluster annotations, spatial gene-expression maps, cell-type proportions, differential expression results, pathway enrichment outputs, quantified staining areas, densitometry of blots, and normalized counts/ratios from qPCR, FACS, and bioluminescence assays. Human snRNA-seq data are analyzed as normalized expression and pathway scores.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly says 'Source data are provided as a Source data file' for multiple figures, implying figure-specific processed data tables underlying the plots. The source data appear to support replotting of the main quantitative figures, but not necessarily the raw images or raw sequencing reads.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: Figure-based source data plus mixed experimental datasets; exact folder structure is not specified.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Quantitative figure values are explicitly said to be provided as source data files, which should allow replotting of many bar plots and summary figures. However, the excerpt does not confirm availability of all raw images, raw sequencing matrices, or analysis scripts, so full end-to-end reproduction is not guaranteed from the text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper mixes several data layers: raw microscopy/cytometry/sequencing measurements, processed spatial transcriptomics and single-cell analyses, and figure-level source data. The strongest evidence for dataset semantics is the repeated statement that source data are provided for figures, while raw-data deposition details are largely absent from the excerpt. The human snRNA-seq analysis is database-based rather than newly generated in this study.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The study generates and analyzes raw experimental measurements including microscopy images, TEM, flow cytometry, western blots, RT-qPCR, metabolic assays, and spatial transcriptomics.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Multiple figure captions explicitly state 'Source data are provided as a Source data file.'
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper clearly contains both raw experimental measurements and figure-level source data for quantitative plots.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Key phrases include 'Source data are provided as a Source data file,' 'Visium dataset integration and cluster annotation using the GSE151658 database,' and 'snRNAseq database analyses confirm...'.
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not confuse figure source data with raw data. The paper clearly provides processed figure data, but the excerpt does not establish deposition of raw sequencing matrices or image files. Human CKD snRNA-seq is an external database analysis, not newly generated raw data in this study.
